summary: Verified Damage/Lost Item Flow for inventory management. All 10 backend tests passed (100%). Frontend UI verified: Report Damage/Lost dialogs, Transfer Item dialog, Distribution tab, Kit Completeness tab. Backend correctly excludes damaged/lost items from distribution counts. Frontend correctly filters active items only for Kit Completeness and Transfer dialogs.

issues: React warning: Missing `Description` or `aria-describedby={undefined}` for {DialogContent}. This is a minor accessibility warning, not a functional issue.

critical_code_review_comments: ✓ Backend /api/items/distribution correctly excludes items with status 'damaged' or 'lost' (line 535: status filter)
✓ Backend POST /api/events with event_type='damage' updates item status to 'damaged' (lines 1007-1011)
✓ Backend POST /api/events with event_type='lost' updates item status to 'lost' (lines 1013-1028)
✓ Frontend Report Damage dialog shows 'Step 2: Select Item' for UNIQUE categories (line 951)
✓ Frontend Report Lost dialog shows 'Step 2: Quantity' for NON-UNIQUE categories (line 976)
✓ Frontend filters items by status === 'active' in Report dialogs (lines 961, 980)
✓ Frontend Transfer dialog filters items by status === 'active' (lines 1196, 1223)
✓ Frontend Kit Completeness filters items by status === 'active' (line 729)
✓ All 12 master categories displayed correctly in Distribution table
✓ Standard Kit Composition reference shows all 12 categories with required quantities
seed_data_creation: Created test items with TEST_DAMAGE_ prefix during backend testing (auto-cleaned up after tests)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Damage/Lost flow fully verified. Key test scenarios: 1) Create item → mark as damaged → verify excluded from distribution, 2) Create item → mark as lost → verify excluded from distribution, 3) UNIQUE categories (laptop, ssd, glove_left, etc.) show item select dropdown in Report dialogs, 4) NON-UNIQUE categories (usb_hub, imu, etc.) show quantity input in Report dialogs, 5) Kit Completeness only counts active items, 6) Transfer dialog only shows active items.
passed_tests: Backend: Damage event updates item status to 'damaged'
Backend: Lost event updates item status to 'lost'
Backend: Items API returns all statuses (for admin management)
Backend: UNIQUE category damage flow (select specific item by ID)
Backend: NON-UNIQUE category lost flow (enter quantity)
Backend: Kit Completeness excludes damaged items
Backend: Transfer only shows active items
Backend: Categories endpoint returns UNIQUE/NON-UNIQUE classification
Frontend: Report Damage button present and opens dialog
Frontend: Report Lost button present and opens dialog
Frontend: Transfer Item button present and opens dialog
Frontend: Distribution tab shows all 12 categories
Frontend: Kit Completeness tab shows Standard Kit Composition reference
Frontend: Kit Completeness shows 13 kit cards with category status
features_verified: Distribution tab shows only active items in the count
Kit Completeness calculation filters items with status 'active' only
Transfer Item dialog only shows items with status 'active' in dropdown
Report Damage dialog (UNIQUE category): select specific item → marks as 'damaged'
Report Lost dialog (NON-UNIQUE category): enter quantity → marks items as 'lost'
After marking item as damaged, it disappears from Distribution count
After marking item as damaged, Kit Completeness shows 'missing' for that category
Damaged items cannot be selected in Transfer Item dialog
